source_jobs.title: Staff Mechanical Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As a Staff Mechanical Design Engineer at REGENT, you will define and drive the design of complex mechanical and actuation systems that are fundamental to how our vehicles move, control, and operate. This role blends deep mechanism design expertise with hands-on development—spanning concept generation, prototype builds, test infrastructure, and production-ready system design.

You will operate across both vehicle systems and test platforms, contributing directly to kinematic system design (e.g., hydrofoil retraction, control surface actuation) while also enabling rapid iteration through the development of test assets, rigs, and demonstrators.

This is a highly cross-functional role working at the intersection of mechanical, electrical, controls, and test engineering. You will bring technical leadership, strong engineering judgement, and a bias toward execution—helping REGENT scale from prototype to production without compromising safety.

If you’re energized by working at the intersection of cutting-edge innovation, real-world impact, and hands-on engineering, you’ll be in the right place - and we’d be thrilled to have you on the team!



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the design and development of complex mechanical and actuation systems across REGENT platforms, including hydrofoil retraction, control surfaces, and motion-critical subsystems.

 - Define system architectures and design approaches for mechanisms involving linkages, transmissions, motors, and integrated motion systems.

 - Design, build, and iterate on test assets, fixtures, and rigs that enable rapid validation and de-risking of new concepts.

 - Translate high-level vehicle requirements into detailed mechanical designs, balancing performance, reliability, manufacturability, and safety.

 - Perform and lead kinematic, dynamic, and structural analyses (hand calculations and FEA) to validate designs and guide trade studies.

 - Partner cross-functionally with electrical, controls, avionics, and test teams to integrate sensors, actuators, and control systems into cohesive solutions.

 - Lead root cause investigations and drive design improvements based on test data and field performance.

 - Develop high-quality CAD models, drawings, and documentation that clearly communicate design intent across teams.

 - Establish best practices for mechanism design, DFMA, tolerance analysis, and system integration as REGENT scales.

 - Mentor engineers and elevate the technical capability of the mechanical team.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Proven experience designing complex mechanical systems involving linkages, gear trains, transmissions, hinges, or similar kinematic systems.

 - Deep understanding of motors, gearboxes, and industrial motion control systems (e.g., servo motors, BLDC systems, drive electronics).

 - Advanced CAD proficiency (NX preferred) with experience designing large assemblies and production-ready components.

 - Strong foundation in engineering analysis including kinematics, force/torque estimation, tolerance analysis, and structural evaluation (FEA).

 - Hands-on experience building, testing, and troubleshooting

source_jobs.title: SkillBridge: Manager, Defense Business Development
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

REGENT is a proud dual-use company with technology that can make a difference to our warfighters, and we need a passionate and experienced defense business development leader to take us to the next level. Reporting directly to the Director of Defense Programs, our Manager, Defense Business Development, will be responsible for refining and executing our defense go-to-market strategy and executing our existing DoD contracts.

The Manager, Defense Business Development, will have three primary initiatives:

 - Long-term strategy: Develop and execute a roadmap to a Program of Record with relevant services

 - Medium-term tactics: Develop and execute strategies for securing opportunities to bolster REGENT’s product development across key defense stakeholders (all services, intelligence community, and innovation units, e.g. DIU)

 - Short-term execution: Ensure flawless execution of our existing Marine Corps and Defense Department programs focused on accelerating full-scale human-carrying seaglider development

Throughout these initiatives, the Manager, Defense Business Development, will work closely with our engineering team to filter and prioritize customer requirements and to communicate technical details, timelines, and program plans to our customers. The Manager, Defense Business Development, will also work closely with the Government Relations function to build and deepen relationships with key government leaders, agencies, and advocacy groups, ensuring REGENT is a leader on legislation and policy matters that are generally important to the business.

Success in this role means our seagliders are built into multiple DoD Programs of Record, comprising a substantial fraction of our total manufacturing capacity. As a result, our nation’s warfighters are better equipped to succeed. If you’re energized by working at the intersection of cutting-edge innovation, real-world impact, and hands-on engineering, you’ll be in the right place - and we’d be thrilled to have you on the team!



REGENT is proud to be a SkillBridge industry partner and offer internship opportunities for transitioning service members. Through this program, you'll gain hands-on experience in the maritime and defense industries, working alongside some of the most respected leaders and engineers in the field. While you continue to receive your military pay and benefits, we’ll provide immersive training and meaningful work that sets you up for success in a civilian career.

To be eligible for a Skillbridge Internship, you must meet the following requirements:

 - Must be eligible to participate in an Internship per DoD Instruction 1322.29

 - Must be projected to receive an honorable discharge from the military

 - Must have all TAP and transition requirements completed prior to the start date

 - Must receive approval from the Unit Commander or O4+ in the chain of command

 - Must be located within a commutable distance or willing/approved to relocate to the job location




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own successful strategy and growth of REGENT’s defense business and team

 - Develop multi-year business development strateg

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, Structures
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As the Senior Manager, Structures, you’ll lead the team responsible for one of the most fundamental aspects of a safe, certifiable vehicle - ensuring our Seaglider can withstand everything it encounters in service, and proving it to the standards required for certification.

You’ll join an existing team of capable engineers and become their functional home: setting technical standards, developing careers, and building a cohesive, disciplined group that programs across REGENT can rely on. You’ll own the quality and consistency of structural analysis, margin documentation, and certification artifacts - not just for one program, but as a function.

Working closely with Loads & Dynamics, Flight Physics, and Manufacturing, you’ll own the structural side of critical cross-functional interfaces: how loads are applied, how margins are calculated, how FEM fidelity is maintained, and how test correlation closes the loop between analysis and reality.

This is a player-coach role. You’ll lead and develop the team while staying technically engaged - doing hands-on work alongside the team and earning credibility through depth, not just authority. If you’re energized by building something rigorous from the inside out, we’d be thrilled to have you on the team.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead and Develop the Structures Team: Own the people side of the Structures function — conducting 1:1s, building development plans, identifying skill gaps, and creating a team identity engineers want to be part of. In a matrix org, you’re their functional home.

 - Establish Structural Analysis Standards: Define and enforce the discipline’s standards for analysis methods, documentation, configuration control, and review processes — ensuring consistent quality and traceability regardless of which program the work supports.

 - Own Structural Substantiation and Certification Approach: Lead the team’s work toward certification-ready artifacts — margin documentation, allowables basis, and compliance approaches for structural substantiation across programs.

 - Drive the Composites Knowledge Base: Own the composites allowables and design-values roadmap — understanding what data exists, what testing is needed, and what interim assumptions are in use. Build the team’s depth in composite laminate design and analysis.

 - Manage Key Cross-Functional Interfaces: Partner with Loads & Dynamics on load application, margining conventions, and FEM integration. Coordinate with Flight Physics on CFD-to-FEM workflows. Work with Manufacturing on design-for-manufacturability and how process constraints feed into structural design.

 - Own Structural Test Planning and Correlation: Define test plans, manage building-block test programs, and own the process for closing the loop between analysis predictions and test results — building a reusable capability the function can apply across programs.

 


WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Structural Engineering Depth: Deep, hands-on expertise in aerospace structural engineering, with a strong background in both composite and metallic structure — someone who can do the work and teach it.

 - Functional

source_jobs.title: Lead Vehicle Test Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As a Lead Vehicle Test Engineer, you’ll own the planning and execution of REGENT’s sea and air test programs for our foiling, wing-in-ground-effect seagliders—turning engineering objectives into safe, rigorous, repeatable test operations. You’ll sit at the intersection of engineering, operations, and safety, building phased test campaigns that reduce risk, expand the envelope, and generate the data needed to mature prototypes into reliable, certifiable transportation systems.

As part of the Vehicle Test team, you’ll be joining a group of driven, collaborative, and talented engineers who take pride in solving complex technical challenges and making an impact as a team. We hold a high bar for engineering rigor, move quickly, think boldly, and rely on our teamwork to push boundaries and bring entirely new technology into the world.

If you thrive in hands-on, high-performance test environments and are motivated by building something fundamentally new in transportation, you’ll be in the right place — and we’d be thrilled to have you on the team!




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Plan and execute test campaigns across sea and air testing—designing phased programs from early on-water trials through flight test and certification-support activities.

 - Develop risk-informed test plans and procedures (test cards, checklists, envelope expansion plans) that systematically reduce risk while advancing capability and performance.

 - Translate engineering requirements into executable tests by partnering with design, loads, controls, structures, and systems to define objectives, success criteria, instrumentation needs, and constraints.

 - Own test readiness and event coordination, ensuring vehicle configuration, personnel, supporting assets, infrastructure, and test environment are aligned for safe execution.

 - Lead and contribute to hazard assessments and safety reviews, identifying hazards, implementing mitigations, and communicating go/no-go logic in high-consequence environments.

 - Support real-time and post-test analysis, monitoring key telemetry during events, capturing anomalies, and producing clear post-test reports that drive decisions and next-step planning.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Experience in vehicle, marine, flight, or integrated prototype testing (aerospace, maritime, automotive, or advanced mobility), with ownership of test events end-to-end.

 - Proven ability to build structured test plans (phased campaigns, test readiness criteria, configuration control, and disciplined documentation) in fast-moving development programs.

 - Strong safety mindset and experience with hazard analysis / risk management (e.g., hazard logs, operational risk assessments, TRRs, safety/airworthiness reviews).

 - Hands-on comfort in the field: working around vehicles, boats, aircraft, high voltage, propulsion systems, or other high-energy systems—calm, decisive, and methodical under pressure.

 - Ability to communicate clearly across technical and non-technical audiences—confident presenting at reviews and aligning cross-functional teams around plan, risk, and execution.

 - Experience leveraging t

source_jobs.title: SkillBridge -  Cybersecurity & IT Operations Associate
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As the SkillBridge - Cybersecurity & IT Operations Associate, you’ll support REGENT’s IT and cybersecurity operations as we scale across RI HQ, the DC office, remote employees, and our upcoming manufacturing facility.

In this hands-on role, you’ll help deploy and maintain secure infrastructure, execute cybersecurity controls aligned to government frameworks (NIST 800-171, CMMC, ITAR), and contribute to reliable day-to-day IT support. You’ll also help strengthen identity and access management, improve vulnerability and patch management, and support incident response readiness.

This is a high-impact opportunity to help protect the systems that keep REGENT moving fast, from everyday employee productivity to the technology that supports our production floor. Your work will directly improve security, reliability, and compliance as we scale from prototype to production and build a new transportation category.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Support Infrastructure Scaling: Assist in the deployment and management of secure networks, cloud services, and endpoints across our HQ, DC office, and upcoming manufacturing facility.

 - Execute Security Controls: Implement technical safeguards and monitoring tools to align with government frameworks like NIST 800-171, CMMC, and ITAR.

 - Identity & Access Management: Manage user lifecycle (onboarding/offboarding) and maintain strict access controls across our SaaS and hardware environments.

 - Operational Excellence: Support the IT helpdesk by triaging tickets, troubleshooting hardware/software issues, and ensuring our distributed team remains productive and secure.

 - Vulnerability Management: Perform regular vulnerability scans, assist with patching schedules, and participate in incident response drills.

 - Manufacturing IT Support: Assist in the setup of IT systems for our production floor, including the segmentation of Operational Technology (OT) from our corporate network.

REGENT is proud to be a SkillBridge industry partner and offer internship opportunities for transitioning service members. Through this program, you'll gain hands-on experience in the maritime and defense industries, working alongside some of the most respected leaders and engineers in the field. While you continue to receive your military pay and benefits, we’ll provide immersive training and meaningful work that sets you up for success in a civilian career.

To be eligible for a Skillbridge Internship, you must meet the following requirements:

 - Must be eligible to participate in an Internship per DoD Instruction 1322.29

 - Must be projected to receive an honorable discharge from the military

 - Must have all TAP and transition requirements completed prior to the start date

 - Must receive approval from the Unit Commander or O4+ in the chain of command

 - Must be located within a commutable distance or willing/approved to relocate to the job location




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Active Duty Status: Must be a transitioning service member eligible for the DOD SkillBridge program https://skillbridge.osd.mil/.

source_jobs.title: Manager, Systems Engineering
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.




ROLE OVERVIEW:

As the Manager of Systems Engineering, you will define how REGENT builds safe, certifiable vehicles at scale.

This is not just about owning systems engineering - it’s about shaping how the entire organization thinks about requirements, safety, and certification. You’ll lead a team that sits at the center of vehicle engineering, helping translate ambitious vehicle concepts into structured, certifiable systems - and ensuring that safety and rigor are embedded from the very beginning.

You’ll coach and develop a growing team of systems engineers, raising the bar for technical judgment, clarity of thinking, and engineering discipline. At the same time, you’ll partner across the company to ensure systems engineering is not a bottleneck, but a force multiplier, enabling teams to move quickly while staying aligned with certification requirements.

Success in this role means building both a strong team and a strong system: engineers who are trusted leaders in the organization, and processes that scale with the company as we move from prototype to production.

If you’re energized by working at the intersection of cutting-edge innovation, real-world impact, and hands-on engineering, you’ll be in the right place - and we’d be thrilled to have you on the team!




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Build and Develop a High-Impact Systems Engineering Team: Lead, coach, and grow a team of systems engineers, investing in their development as both technical experts and cross-functional leaders. Create clarity around expectations, provide regular feedback, and help the team scale with the needs of the program.

 - Make Systems Engineering a Force Multiplier Across the Company: Embed systems thinking into the broader engineering organization - helping teams understand how to define requirements, reason about risk, conduct verification and validation, and contribute to certification. Ensure systems engineering enables progress rather than slowing it down.

 - Drive Safety and Certification from the Start: Ensure system safety and certification artifacts are developed early and used to actively guide design decisions. Establish a culture where safety analyses and requirements are foundational inputs, not after-the-fact validations.

 - Define and Scale the Right Processes: Build, utilize, and refine systems engineering and system safety processes that are rigorous enough for certification, but practical enough for a fast-moving development environment. Continuously improve how the team operates as the company grows.

 - Own System Definition and Requirements Quality: Guide and contribute to the development of clear, well-structured requirements across all levels of the vehicle, ensuring alignment across disciplines and traceability to certification needs.

 - Lead Risk-Based System Safety Work: Oversee and contribute to analyses such as FHA, FTA, and FMEA, ensuring they are integrated into design decisions and reflect a deep understanding of system-level risk.

 - Partner with Regulators and Certification Stakeholders: Work closely with internal teams and external regulators to define and deliver certifica

source_jobs.title: Chief Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As Chief Engineer, you will be the vehicle-level technical authority for REGENT’s Viceroy Seaglider program- the single person responsible for the coherence, integration, and technical integrity of the entire vehicle system.

This is not a functional management role. It’s the role that sits across the disciplines- spanning structures, aerodynamics, propulsion, systems, and controls - and ensures that every design decision, interface, and assumption fits together into a vehicle that works. You will partner directly with the CTO and Program Manager, driving the vehicle toward PDR and CDR with a clear and defensible technical picture.

You’ll lead cross-functional integration reviews, adjudicate trade studies, and resolve interface conflicts that no single discipline can own. You’ll be the person who ensures that Viceroy’s design is not just technically sound in each discipline, but coherent and certifiable as a system.

Success means the program operates from a single, authoritative technical baseline—understood by every functional lead, credible to certification stakeholders, and ready to support PDR, CDR, and certification. It also means serving as a trusted technical leader and mentor: strengthening engineering judgment, guiding cross-functional decision-making, and helping teams solve hard problems with clarity and rigor. If you’re energized by building something genuinely new and operating at the center of a complex, fast-moving program, we’d love to meet you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own Vehicle-Level Technical Integration: Serve as the vehicle-level technical authority for Viceroy. Drive cross-functional integration reviews, resolve interface conflicts between disciplines, and ensure design decisions spanning structures, aerodynamics, propulsion, systems, and controls are coherent, documented, and defensible.

 - Drive Design Maturity Toward PDR and CDR: Define what “PDR-ready” and “CDR-ready” mean in terms of design maturity, analysis completeness, risk posture, and documentation. Lead the program’s technical preparation for major design gates.

 - Decompose and Control Vehicle-Level Requirements: Own the technical decomposition of vehicle-level specifications into subsystem and discipline-level requirements. Maintain configuration control of the vehicle-level baseline and manage how changes propagate across the system.

 - Lead Trade Studies and Technical Decision-Making: Drive the trade studies and technical decisions that span multiple disciplines. Ensure design choices are made with full visibility into system-wide implications - including weight, performance, structural, and certification impacts.

 - Define the Certification Technical Strategy: In partnership with the certification team, own the vehicle-level compliance approach - mapping design choices to the certification basis and ensuring technical decisions are structured to support verification.

 - Enable Cross-Functional Technical Teams: Partner with functional managers to ensure each discipline has clear interface definitions, consistent assumptions, and the technical direction they need to execute. Enable

source_jobs.title: Staff Structures Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As a Staff Structures Engineer, you’ll own critical composite structural analyses that help REGENT deliver flight-capable seaglider hardware safely and on schedule. You’ll drive sizing, margins, and trade studies across key load cases, support certification-focused analysis and documentation, and help unblock analysis throughput as we scale from prototype to product across multiple programs.

As part of the Structures team, you’ll be joining a group of driven, collaborative, and talented engineers who take pride in solving complex technical challenges and making an impact as a team. We hold a high bar for engineering rigor, move quickly, think boldly, and rely on our teamwork to push boundaries and bring entirely new technology into the world.

If you’re energized by working at the intersection of cutting-edge innovation, real-world impact, and hands-on engineering, you’ll be in the right place — and we’d be thrilled to have you on the team!




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Execute composite structural analysis for flight-capable hardware across key load cases (static, buckling, modal, fatigue) and translate results into clear design guidance.

 - Own margins and substantiation for primary and secondary structure, partnering with design to close gaps and drive weight-efficient solutions.

 - Deliver high-impact analyses supporting major milestones.

 - Develop and maintain analysis models, assumptions, and documentation that enable repeatable, reviewable engineering decisions.

 - Collaborate closely with loads, manufacturing, and certification stakeholders to ensure analyses align with requirements, producibility, and verification needs.

 - Contribute to improving team execution by helping refine procedures, templates, and best practices that reduce analysis bottlenecks.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Experienced structures engineer with significant focus in composite structures analysis.

 - Strong FEM experience (static, buckling, modal, fatigue) with the ability to interpret composite-specific failure modes and margins.

 - Experience managing global vehicle FEM models.

 - Working knowledge of composite materials and processes (prepreg, RTM, infusion, autoclave, OOA) and how they influence allowables and structural behavior.

 - Solid fundamentals in laminate theory, load paths, and structural sizing across the product lifecycle for flight-capable hardware.

 - Familiarity with requirements, loads/margins workflows, and certification-oriented analysis and documentation practices.

 - Clear written and verbal communication skills, plus a collaborative, open-minded approach in fast-moving, interdisciplinary teams.





WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Collaborative Team Player: You believe that great things happen when teams work together. You communicate effectively and collaborate with engineers, stakeholders, and other cross-functional teams to align on goals and move projects forward. You bring people together, ensuring that everyone is on the same page and moving towards a shared vision.

 - Creative Problem Solver: You thrive on tackling complex challenges and finding innovative solution

source_jobs.title: Director, Hardware Engineering
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As Director, Hardware Engineering, you’ll lead REGENT’s hardware engineering organization through its next critical phase - stepping into a team with real momentum and helping evolve it into the high-performing, process-mature organization that can carry Seaglider programs from concept through certification and into production. You’re joining a sizable organization with strong technical talent and real momentum already in motion. This is not a blank-slate build - the foundations are in place, and the next chapter is about strengthening leadership, sharpening execution, and building the systems needed to scale. Your first priority will be to listen, assess, and earn trust: understand where the team is strongest, where friction exists, and what support leaders and engineers need to perform at their best.

From that foundation, you’ll lead the next stage of growth - strengthening the organization where it matters most and building the capabilities required for scale. A core part of the role is coaching and developing functional managers into exceptional leaders: helping them raise performance, grow their teams, navigate tough decisions, and lead with greater clarity and accountability. You’ll build leadership capacity while creating an environment where managers can operate with ownership and consistency. You’ll also raise the bar on execution, mature the processes that enable complex hardware programs to move faster with less risk, and build the cross-functional partnerships that make the broader engineering organization more effective.

As part of Engineering leadership, you’ll co-own REGENT’s engineering operating system: shared standards for configuration management, design reviews, requirements traceability, and documentation that underpin both hardware and software delivery. Working closely with Chief Engineers and PMO, you’ll help ensure the hardware organization delivers consistently across multiple programs.

Success looks like an organization that is stronger, more cohesive, and more scalable than when you arrived - with stronger managers, improved systems, and a team ready to execute through certification and beyond. If you’re energized by inheriting something strong and helping make it exceptional, we’d love to meet you!




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead and Strengthen the Hardware Engineering Organization: Own the people, structure, and culture of a 30+ person (and growing!) hardware engineering organization spanning structures, aerodynamics, propulsion, electrical, and mechanical systems. Build on the strong technical foundation already in place while strengthening leadership, raising expectations, and creating a culture of accountability, rigor, and ownership.

 - Coach and Develop Functional Managers: Invest deeply in the growth of functional managers and emerging leaders. Coach them to lead higher-performing teams, make stronger decisions, navigate complexity, and operate with greater ownership, consistency, and accountability.

 - Own Delivery and Execution Rhythm: Establish clear operating cadences, decision-making mechanisms, and accountability systems that keep wo

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.



Position Title: Senior Software Systems Engineer

Company/Employer: REGENT Craft Inc.

Worksite Location: 200 Callahan Road, North Kingstown, RI 02852



JOB DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

1. Responsible for improving, reviewing, and testing Vehicle Control System (VCS) software architecture, specifically in regard to application-level functionality and integration with our real-time operating system capabilities.

2. Developing VCS application software components in C++

3. Systems engineering work (requirements, test definition, architectural improvements, interfacing with other engineering stakeholders)

4. Designing, Developing and Improving Test Automation Infrastructure and Tools in Python.



MINIMUM EDUCATION/EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS: 

 - Bachelor’s degree or foreign degree equivalent in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, or directly related field of study. Employer also requires the following:
   
   1. 3 years experience designing, developing, and testing embedded software for time and resource-constrained applications
   
   2. 3 years experience programming in C++ and C in a Linux environment
   
   3. 3 years safety-critical software experience in aerospace, medical, automotive, or similar applications
   
   4. 3 years experience in object-oriented design principles
   
   5. 3 years experience developing software on resource-constrained Real Time Operating Systems (RTOS) and configuring RTOSs such as FreeRTOS, DEOS, VxWorks, or Integrity
   
   6. 3 years experience scripting experience in Python, MATLAB, or Bash
   
   7. 1 year development experience with Publish or Subscribe architectures for Distributed Software Systems
   
   8. 1 year experience interpreting and reviewing electrical schematics
   
   9. 1 year experience working with common electrical lab equipment such as Oscilloscopes, Multimeters, and Signal Generators.

 - Hybrid position. Must reside within commuting distance from employer's North Kingstown, RI office (3 days a week in office minimum).



If you’re ready to take on an exciting challenge, grow with us, and make a lasting impact, we want to hear from you. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, but you see yourself contributing to our mission, we encourage you to apply! We value diverse skillsets and experiences and believe in the potential of passionate individuals to make a big impact.

Equal Opportunity Employer

REGENT is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, or any other characteristic protected by law.

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, Loads & Dynamics
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.



ROLE OVERVIEW:

As the Senior Manager, Loads & Dynamics, you will build and lead the function that defines the forces our vehicles must withstand - and ensures those definitions are technically sound, internally consistent, and certifiable.

This role sits at the foundation of vehicle integrity. You’ll establish the methodologies, assumptions, and datasets that drive structural design, inform flight physics, and underpin certification. Today, these responsibilities are distributed across teams; your role is to bring them into a coherent, defensible system.

You’ll start as a player-coach: setting direction, doing hands-on technical work, and building the team over time. You’ll work across Structures, Flight Physics, and Certification to align on a single source of truth for loads - resolving ambiguity, closing gaps, and ensuring the program is built on a solid and scalable foundation.

Success in this role means the organization is operating from a single, trusted loads basis - clearly defined, consistently applied, and ready to support both structural design and certification - supported by a strong, scalable Loads & Dynamics team you’ve built and developed.

If you’re energized by working at the intersection of cutting-edge innovation, real-world impact, and hands-on engineering, you’ll be in the right place - and we’d be thrilled to have you on the team!




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Build and Lead the Loads & Dynamics Function: Establish the team from the ground up - defining standards, operating cadence, and technical expectations - while hiring, mentoring, and developing engineers. Act as a player-coach, contributing directly while scaling the team.

 - Define the Vehicle Loads Basis: Develop a coherent, traceable set of load cases across all operating modes and environmental conditions. Establish the operating envelope and ensure loads assumptions are consistent, controlled, and clearly communicated across the program.

 - Own Dynamics and Aero/Hydroelastic Approach: Define how the program addresses flutter risk, modal behavior, and dynamic response, including analysis methods, GVT strategy, and how coupling effects are incorporated into loads and verification.

 - Establish Loads Validation and Integration: Define how simulation, structural models, and test data come together. Build the processes for CFD-to-loads mapping, FEM integration, and correlation between predicted and measured loads.

 - Enable Certification Through Defensible Methods: Develop the compliance approach for loads and dynamics, including assumptions, traceability, and required evidence. Produce certification-ready artifacts and support technical discussions with regulators and classification bodies.

 - Drive Cross-Functional Alignment: Partner with Structures and Flight Physics to define clear interfaces, resolve gaps, and maintain a single source of truth for loads across the organization.
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL BRING:

 - Deep Loads & Dynamics Expertise: Significant experience defining loads for complex aerospace, maritime, or hybrid vehicles, including ownership of load cases used for structural design and certifi

source_jobs.title: Seaglider Test Operator
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As a Seaglider Test Operator, you will serve as the primary test crew member (Captain, Watch Officer) to safely execute on-water sea and air trials and demonstrations of prototype and production Seagliders. You'll plan and execute engineering sea and air trials and vehicle demonstrations, including initial envelope expansion and vehicle flights domestic and international customer and investor - leading fellow operators within the test operations team, ensuring adequate training of all operators, developing foundational operational documents and test cards, executing test plans, performing risk assessments, and coordinating with external stakeholders.

This is a commercial developmental test program moving at startup speed. You'll help write the playbook with our team: building procedures, checklists, and training materials from scratch while executing tests safely and efficiently. Pace expectations are closer to an aerospace startup than a government program - and we're looking for someone energized by that.

You'll work closely with engineers to define test objectives and feedback results, with business development and marketing teams to plan demonstrations, and with the broader test team on day-to-day operations.

If you've ever wanted to be one of the first people to fly a genuinely new category of vehicle - and help define how it's tested and operated - this is that role.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Ensure safe execution of on-water sea trials and demonstrations of prototype and production Seagliders across all operators

 - Operate the vehicle for Seaglider envelope expansion and first flight events

 - Plan and execute engineering sea trials and domestic and international customer and investor demonstrations.

 - Lead the development of test cards, risk assessments, and weekly/month test schedules; coordinate with external stakeholders.

 - Build and refine crew procedures, checklists, and crew training courses and materials from the ground up.

 - Provide feedback on cockpit user interfaces and ergonomics to improve Seaglider operation.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Demonstrated experience as a test pilot or in developmental flight testing of novel, experimental, or commercial aircraft.

 - Strong foundation in engineering, computer science, math, flight sciences, naval architecture, or a related technical discipline - however you built it.

 - Demonstrated knowledge of novel or prototype vehicle operations, including risk/hazard identification and mitigation.

 - Experience with aircraft or boat handling, development or operational test, and demonstration planning.

 - Startup mindset: willing to build processes from scratch, move fast, and wear multiple hats.

 - Must be willing to relocate to Rhode Island (*we offer a staggered relo program) and travel to support demonstrations.




YOU'RE THE KIND OF PERSON WHO...

 - Thrives at the edge of the envelope - literally. You're energized by being one of the first to operate a new category of vehicle, not intimidated by it.

 - Builds as you go. You're comfortable writing the checklist that didn't exist yesterday and flying from

source_jobs.title: Embedded Systems Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As an Embedded Systems Software Engineer, you’ll play a role in developing and certifying our Seaglider product family. You’ll contribute to embedded software development, simulation testing, and hands-on electronics work, helping ensure our platform stays reliable, testable, and ready to evolve.

From managing real-time software and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing to wiring up harnesses and spinning up GUIs, your work will directly impact how we prototype, validate, and push the boundaries of our tech. You’ll also collaborate closely with our controls, avionics, and modeling teams to ensure a smooth path from concept to demo - both on the bench and at sea. If you’re energized by working at the intersection of cutting-edge innovation, real-world impact, and hands-on engineering, you’ll be in the right place - and we’d be thrilled to have you on the team!




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Contribute to Seaglider product family embedded software stack: Develop, debug, and maintain the real-time software that runs on our Seaglider product family, from microcontroller code to Linux-based applications.

 - Support test environment development and testing: Contribute towards development of the SIL and HIL test environment for verification of controls software. This will include coordinating with multiple disciplinary teams.

 - Manage COTS electronics & harnessing: Set up and maintain the electronics ecosystem - from sensors and servos to single-board computers, harnesses, and breakout boards.

 - Improve developer tools and processes: Automate build systems, streamline deployment pipelines, and build tooling to facilitate maintenance and certification.

 - Prototype and field-test: Be a key part of the sea trials team - monitoring operation, running diagnostics, troubleshooting in the field, and reliably showing off our latest and greatest Seaglider capabilities.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Demonstrated experience writing C++ for embedded systems - whether on Linux, RTOS, or bare metal

 - Strong hands-on experience with microcontrollers and common embedded interfaces (Serial, SPI, CAN, PWM, ADCs, DIO, etc.)

 - Ability to read, interpret, and contribute to wiring diagrams and electrical schematics

 - Comfortable scripting with Python, MATLAB, or similar languages




BONUS EXPERIENCE

 - Experience with LabVIEW or Simulink for DAQ and HIL systems

 - Familiarity with actuation systems like servo motors

 - Field-testing experience and a knack for troubleshooting electronics in real-world environments

 - Experience building safety-critical applications

 - Familiarity with aerospace or automotive standards like DO-178C or ISO 26262




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Collaborative Team Player: You believe that great things happen when teams work together. You communicate effectively and collaborate with engineers, stakeholders, and other cross-functional teams to align on goals and move projects forward. You bring people together, ensuring that everyone is on the same page and moving towards a shared vision.

 - Creative Problem Solver: You thrive on tackling complex challenges and finding inno

source_jobs.title: Maritime Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As Maritime Operations Manager, you will lead and grow REGENT's Shore Operations Team – responsible for Seaglider test support, vessel and equipment operations, crane and lift operations, and on-water safety. This is a build role: establish training programs, build the internal capability to safely and efficiently execute Seaglider testing.

You will be the leader of maritime operations — from pre-test coordination through execution, to sustaining a world-class operations organization. Before each test event, you'll drive the planning, readiness reviews, and cross-functional alignment that set the team up to succeed. On test days, you'll be the operational authority on site — integrating with Test Ops, Engineering, and Program Management in real time, commanding launches and recoveries, and making go/no-go calls. Beyond individual test events, you'll own the longer arc: building the team, establishing the training and certification infrastructure, and scaling Shore Operations into a function that can support higher-frequency testing, remote demonstrations, and the full lifecycle of REGENT's Seaglider program.

If you're energized by building and leading a high-performing operations team in a fast-moving, high-consequence test environment, we'd be thrilled to have you on ours.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead Day-of-Test Operations: Serve as the operational lead on test days — coordinating Seaglider launches and recoveries, chase boats, crane/lift operations, and on-water safety. Make and own go/no-go decisions. Integrate in real time with Test Ops, Engineering, and Program Management to enable safe, efficient test execution. When you aren’t present, have trained capable individuals to lead operationally.

 - Own On-Water Safety: Design, maintain, and drill the team on emergency action plans and on-water safety procedures. Build a proactive safety culture that holds regardless of schedule pressure, and that the team can execute without you in the room.

 - Build and Develop the Team: Hire, onboard, and develop a Shore Operations team. Establish the training and certification pipeline — vessel operations, crane/lift, emergency response, and Seaglider-specific procedures. Set a high performance bar and hold it.

 - Manage the Maritime Fleet: Own the day-to-day readiness of REGENT's support vessel fleet and dock equipment — maintenance schedules, vendor and marina relationships, safety equipment, and test-day logistics. Keep the fleet mission-ready.

 - Scale Shore Operations: Build the processes, documentation, and team depth that allow Shore Ops to support remote demonstrations, higher-frequency testing, and product qualification.




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Operational Leadership Experience: Experienced in leading marine operations teams in high-stakes, high-tempo environments — vehicle test programs, military small craft, flight test, or similar programs where you were the person running operations, not just supporting them.

 - Team Building Track Record: Led hiring and developing hands-on technical or operations personnel. Has built training and certification programs, established

source_jobs.title: Government Capture and Mission Assurance Manager
source_jobs.description:
At REGENT, we’re on a mission to revolutionize regional transportation between coastal cities. We're not just building vehicles, we're creating a new category of transportation: the Seaglider. These groundbreaking wing-in-ground-effect craft travel just above the water’s surface, combining the speed of an airplane with the low operating costs of a boat. Capable of 180 miles at 180 mph on today's battery tech (and up to 400 miles with next-gen batteries), Seagliders will unlock fast, affordable, and sustainable coastal transit for passengers and cargo - connecting cities, islands, and communities like never before.

Backed by leading investors and powered by a team of passionate, forward-thinking engineers, builders, and operators, we’re now scaling from a breakthrough prototype to full production - and building the team to make it happen.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As Government Capture and Mission Assurance Manager, you will report to the Director, Defense Mission Integration and partner closely with cross-functional teams to win and execute government-funded work. You’ll own the end-to-end capture and proposal process for prioritized state and federal opportunities—building a qualified pipeline, shaping win strategies, driving bid/no-bid decisions, and leading proposal development through on-time, compliant submission.

In parallel, you’ll establish a practical mission assurance and compliance rhythm that keeps REGENT audit-ready without slowing execution. This includes translating contract and regulatory requirements into clear internal plans, maintaining a mission assurance/compliance register with owners and due dates, and building an evidence-based documentation approach that scales as work grows.

Success in this role looks like building repeatable systems: a pipeline review cadence with clear next steps, a proposal production process with structured reviews and compliance checks, and a focused plan to close priority compliance gaps for active and near-term work.

If you’re energized by working at the intersection of cutting-edge innovation and real-world impact, you’ll be in the right place - and we’d be thrilled to have you on the team!




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Capture & Proposals
   
   - Identify, track, and qualify opportunities across DoD and non-DoD sources; synthesize requirements, competitive context, and recommended pursuit strategy
   
   - Lead bid/no-bid recommendations with clear rationale (strategic fit, resourcing, risks, probability of win, timeline)
   
   - Build proposal schedules, assign owners, and drive execution through submission
   
   - Coordinate cross-functional inputs (technical, finance, legal, program, security/IT) and turn them into clear, compliant proposal narratives
   
   - Create and maintain core artifacts (capture plan, proposal outline, compliance matrix, review plan, lessons learned)

 - Mission Assurance/Compliance
   
   - Translate contract and regulatory requirements (e.g., FAR/DFARS, ITAR, cybersecurity, reporting) into an internal implementation plan with owners and deadlines
   
   - Maintain audit-ready documentation (policies, evidence, training records) and drive closure on gaps through partnering teams
   
   - Plan and run periodic internal assessments/audits and document findings, remediation plans, and status updates
   
   - Stay current on relevant regulatory changes and incorporate updates into internal processes and guidance

 - Stakeholder Engagement
   
   - Represent REGENT with government stakeholders and partners at targeted industry events to support capture objectives (relationship-building, intel gathering, teaming)
   
   - Support long-term readiness efforts (e.g., EVMS compliance roadmap, facility clearance process) in partnership with internal owners




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Bachelor’s degree in Political Science, Business Administration, Marketing, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience)

 - 3+ years of experience in government capture/prop

notes: Highly technical roles, with a focus on engineering and design. No explicit mention of sponsorship or clearance requirements.
